campbell.title: Campbell City Council — April 21, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to discuss updating the city's business license tax rates for the upcoming fiscal year. Shannon Dragman from the Finance Department presented the proposed changes to the tax code, and the council debated the matter in public before voting on whether to approve the new rates.
keyTopics: Business license tax rate increases
Municipal code amendments for fiscal year 2026
Finance department presentation and council deliberation
County emergency preparedness coordination

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 to get community input on a new rule or policy. After hearing from residents, the council moved forward by skipping the usual first step and planning to vote on adopting the new ordinance at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing conducted to gather resident feedback
New ordinance fast-tracked for adoption
Vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
First reading waived to expedite the process

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 21, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 21, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from earlier in the month and review the town's finances for February. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign several contracts for construction services and professional agreements to keep town projects and operations moving forward.
keyTopics: Approved previous meeting minutes from April 7
Reviewed February 2026 financial and investment reports
Authorized construction contracts with QLM, Inc. and other vendors
Approved a three-year professional services agreement
Town improvements project planning initiated

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 5, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to approve meeting minutes and discuss several projects affecting the city. Main items included replacing an elevator at a terminal building, hiring companies to clean up homeless encampments, updating an agreement with a digital advertising company for billboards on city property, and approving a firefighter memorial event.
keyTopics: Terminal Elevator Replacement Project
Encampment Cleanup Services - Request for Proposals
Digital Signage Agreement with Clear Channel Outdoor
Light the Night Firefighter Memorial Event approval and funding

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council met to approve previous meeting minutes and hear directly from residents during public comment. The main focus was reviewing and deciding on funding for neighborhood grant requests for the 2026-27 fiscal year, along with discussing how often council members are attending neighborhood meetings.
keyTopics: Neighborhood grant funding decisions for next fiscal year
Public comment period for residents
Council attendance at neighborhood meetings
Approval of previous meeting minutes

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 28, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special evening meeting on April 28, 2026, to interview candidates for various city boards and commissions. Residents had the opportunity to provide public comment either in person or online during the session.
keyTopics: Board and commission candidate interviews
Public comment period (in-person and online options)
Special study session format

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 5, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a closed-door meeting to negotiate the purchase or sale of property at 10480 Finch Avenue with the Cupertino Union School District. City Manager Tina Kapoor and City Attorney Floy Andrews represented the city in discussions about the price and payment terms for this real estate deal. The council also discussed the Silicon Valley Hopper program and future grant funding opportunities.
keyTopics: Real estate negotiation for 10480 Finch Avenue property with the school district
Price and payment terms being discussed between the city and school district
Silicon Valley Hopper program overview and grant funding strategy

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 21, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council met to discuss allowing large decorative signs to be displayed on buildings around Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The council also approved a new labor agreement with firefighters that will run through 2029.
keyTopics: Temporary large signs permitted near Levi's Stadium for World Cup 2026
New firefighter contract agreement for 2026-2029
One agenda item was withdrawn by staff before the meeting

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to plan the city's finances for the next five years and discuss how to spend money in 2026-27. They also approved a plan for new parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to how the council conducts its meetings.
keyTopics: 5-year capital budget plan for 2027-2031
2026-27 operating budget presentation
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan approval
Updates to City Council meeting procedures

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 29, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a meeting on April 29, 2026, to discuss a proposal to divide a vacant 1-acre lot on Old Trace Road into nine residential lots and add nine new single-family homes plus seven smaller accessory units. The council also handled routine business like electing leadership and reviewing the director's report and meeting schedule.
keyTopics: Subdivision of Old Trace Road property into 9 residential lots
Construction of 9 single-family homes and 7 junior accessory dwelling units
Creation of private street to serve new homes
Election of council chair and vice chair
Project approved as exempt from environmental review under state law
